log_entries.thought_preview: The malware is a PE32 executable with FTP communication and persistence capabilities, and it has been analyzed in multiple sandboxes. Investigating the FTP communication and persistence mechanisms cou

The malware has dropped two files, created one process, and used the SetFileTime API call. It also has two registry persistence keys. Investigating the dropped files and registry persistence keys coul

The malware has dropped two files, jusched.exe and C:\Program Files (x86)\71d2c87e\jusched.exe, with different SHA256 hashes. This suggests that the malware may be using a technique to evade detection

The malware is communicating with the IP address 209.202.252.54 on port 21, which is a typical FTP port. The DNS lookups suggest that the malware is trying to resolve the domains dictio_802884.di.funp
The malware exhibits various capabilities, including calculating modulo 256, delaying execution, enumerating files on Windows, and scheduling tasks via ITaskScheduler. These capabilities suggest that
The malware creates a process with the path C:\Program Files\6c78a9fa\jusched.exe and loads several notable modules, including api-ms-win-core-localregistry-l1-1-0.dll and rasman.dll. The highlighted

The malware creates two registry persistence keys, HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\SharedAccess\Parameters\FirewallPolicy\StandardProfile\AuthorizedApplications\List\C:\Program Files (x86)\71d2
